metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_763
metadata.comment:
Rationale: The paper reports the identification of the GABA transporters GAT-3 and GAT-4.

Figure 2 demonstrates the uptake Kinetics of 3H-GABA (2A), 3H-beta-alanine (2B), and 3H-taurine (3C) into Xenopus oocytes injected with synthetic mRNA of GAT3.
This suggests that GAT3 is the transporter of GABA, beta-alanine, and taurine.

rule: NAS_MFCC-BP
rationale:
Protein has "gamma-aminobutyric acid transmembrane transporter activity (GO:0015185)" and is localized in the presynaptic membrane (show with high-resolution technique). The inference to term 'neurotransmitter uptake (GO:0001504)' is trivial and non-controversial.
Pinf_CC:
[P31647] SynGO ID 768 (PMID:24368619, EM). Experimental description: 'Kinetics of GABA, and alanine, and taurine uptake into Xenopus oocytes injected with synthetic mRNA of GAT3'
mRNA is isolated from mouse cDNA library.

checks.label: OWL Consistency
